plan.description:
The research is aimed to measure and describe the features of the monetary transmission mechanism in stable and critical periods for a set of economies.
It features the aggregated cross-sectional data from a number of international organizations in comparison with the data from the National Bank of Ukraine
plan.title: Measures of the monetary transmission efficiency

systemField.label: Description
systemField.description: Briefly describe the context and purpose of the DMP

systemField.label: Language
systemField.description: Select the language of your DMP

referenceField.label: Researchers
referenceField.description: Add here the names of people that have produced, processed, analysed the data described in the DMP.

referenceField.label: Organizations
referenceField.description: Add here the names of the organizations contributing to the creation and revision of the DMPs

referenceField.label: Funding organizations
referenceField.description: Select a funder of your research or add new

referenceField.label: Grants
referenceField.description: Find the grant of your research or add new

referenceField.label: Project
referenceField.description: Projects in Argos are perceived as distinct activities falling under a grant or common activities under different grants in collaborative schemas, eg open call for contributions. Please complete it for the grant associated to your organization if your project falls under this category. In all other cases, please leave blank and it will be autocompleted.

systemField.label: Access Rights
systemField.description: Choose how the DMP is displayed after is published on Zenodo. By choosing Open Access, the DMP will be open on Zenodo after the Publication Date. By choosing Restricted Access, the DMP will be restricted after the publication is made.

referenceField.label: License
referenceField.description: Assign a license to your DMP by selecting the most appropriate from the list.

reference.label: National University of Kyiv-Mohyla Academy Doctoral Programme

description.description: This research examines the effectiveness of inflation targeting as a monetary policy strategy in conditions of heightened economic uncertainty. By analyzing empirical evidence across varying contexts, it evaluates the stability, adaptability, and outcomes of inflation-targeting frameworks, highlighting their efficiency in achieving price stability and mitigating adverse economic impacts during uncertain times.
description.label: COMPARING THE EVIDENCE OF INFLATION TARGETING EFFICIENCY UNDER HIGH UNCERTAINTY

tags.tag: Inflation targeting, emerging economies, central bank, policy.

descriptionTemplate.label: UK Research Innovation
descriptionTemplate.description: Dataset description template for UK Research Innovation

fieldSet.title: What is the purpose of the data collection/generation and its relation to the objectives of the project?
fieldSet.extendedDescription: Up to three lines of text that summarise the type of study (or studies) for which the data are being collected.

fieldSet.title: What is the origin of the data?
fieldSet.extendedDescription: Types of data to be managed in the following terms: quantitative, qualitative; generated from surveys, clinical measurements, interviews, medical records, electronic health records, administrative records, genotypic data, images, tissue samples...

fieldSet.title: What types and formats of data will the project generate/collect?
fieldSet.extendedDescription: File formats, software used, number of records, databases, sweeps, repetitions… (in terms that are meaningful in your field). Do formats and software enable sharing and long-term validity of data?

section.title: Format and scale of the data

section.description: Make sure you justify why new data collection or long term management is needed in your Case for Support. Focus in this template on the good practice and standards for ensuring new data are of high quality and processing is well documented
section.title: Data collection / generation

fieldSet.title: What internationally recognised licence will you use for your data?
Will you use naming conventions for your data?

labelData.label: URL where this information can be found
Name of the conventions used for the data

fieldSet.title: URL/Name

section.description: How the data will be collected/generated and which community data standards (if any) will be used at this stage.
section.title: Methodologies for data collection / generation

referenceTypeData.label: Select a vocabulary from the list

fieldSet.title: Will you use standardised vocabularies?

labelData.label: URL where the vocabularies of the data can be found
Briefly describe selected vocabularies application for your needs

fieldSet.title: URL/Description
Will you use standardised formats for some or all of your data?
Which standardised data formats do you plan on using?

labelData.label: URL where this information can be found

fieldSet.title: Standardised formats
fieldSet.extendedDescription: Is the structure of the file(s) provided in a standardised format?

fieldSet.title: Please describe the formats you plan to store your data in, including any URLs to documentation.
Will you provide metadata describing the quality of the data?

section.description: How consistency and quality of data collection / generation will be controlled and documented, through processes of calibration, repeat samples or measurements, standardised data capture or recording, data entry validation, peer review of data or representation with controlled vocabularies.
section.title: Data quality and standards

fieldSet.title: Briefly describe how data will be stored, backed-up, managed and curated in the short to medium term. Specify any community agreed or other formal data standards used (with URL references).

referenceTypeData.label: Select a metadata standard from the list

fieldSet.title: Will you use metadata to describe the data?

labelData.label: URL where metadata can be found
Name of repository, database, platform that holds the data

fieldSet.title: URL/Location
Will your metadata be harvestable?

section.description: What metadata is produced about the data generated from the research/innovation? For example, descriptions of data that enable research/innovation data to be used by others outside of your own team. This may include documenting the methods used to generate the data, analytical and procedural information, capturing instrument metadata alongside data, documenting provenance of data and their coding, detailed descriptions for variables, records,etc.
section.title: Metadata standards and data documentation

page.title: Data security and confidentiality of potentially disclosive information

section.description: This section MUST be completed if your data includes personal data relating to human participants. For other research/innovation, the safeguarding and security of data should also be considered. Information provided will be in line with your ethical review. Please note this section concerns protecting the data, not the patients.
section.title: Data security and confidentiality of potentially disclosive information

fieldSet.title: If your organisation is ISO compliant? If yes, please state the registration number.
Identify formal information standards with which your study is or will be compliant.

selectData.label: Select from the list of answers

options.label: Kept on secure, managed storage for limited time
Kept on insecure, unmanaged storage for limited time

fieldSet.title: What do you plan to do with research data of limited use

labelData.label: Provide additional information or justification about your selection

fieldSet.title: Please describe why the data will be kept on insecure, unmanaged storage

section.description: All personal data has an element of risk. Summarise the main risks to the confidentiality and security of information related to human participants, the level of risk and how these risks will be managed. Cover the main processes or facilities for storage and processing of personal data, data access, with controls put in place and any auditing of user compliance with consent and security conditions. It is not sufficient to write not applicable under this heading
section.title: Main risks to data security

referenceTypeData.label: Select the repository where your data will be uploaded

fieldSet.title: How will the data be made available?
fieldSet.extendedDescription: Identify any data repository (-ies) that are, or will be, entrusted with storing, curating and/or sharing data from your study, where they exist for particular disciplinary domains or data types.

fieldSet.title: URL/Name
Will all your data be openly accessible?
fieldSet.extendedDescription: Is the data you propose to collect (or existing data you propose to use) in the study suitable for sharing? If yes, briefly state why it is suitable. If No, indicate why the data will not be suitable for sharing and then go to Section 6.

fieldSet.title: Data type/ Reason/ URL
Data type/ Reason/ URL
As part of the consent process, proposed procedures for data sharing should be set out clearly and current and potential future risks associated with this explained to participants.

section.description: Restriction to data sharing may be due to participant confidentiality, consent agreements or IPR. Strategies to limit restrictions may include data being anonymised or aggregated; gaining participant consent for data sharing; gaining copyright permissions. For prospective studies, consent procedures should include provision for data sharing to maximise the value of the data for wider research/innovation use, while providing adequate safeguards for participants.
section.title: Restrictions or delays to sharing, with planned actions to limit such restrictions

fieldSet.title: Are there any methods or tools required to access the data?

labelData.label: Briefly explain the method need to be applied for data access
URL where the method can be found

fieldSet.title: Please provide links describing the methods for accessing the data.

labelData.label: Briefly describe the tool needed to be used for data access
URL where the tool can be found

fieldSet.title: Please provide links describing the tools for accessing the data.
How widely accessible is this depository? Indicate whether your policy or approach to data sharing is (or will be) published on your study website (or by other means).

section.description: Indicate how potential new users (outside of your organisation) can find out about your data and identify whether it could be suitable for their research/innovation purposes, e.g. through summary information (metadata) being readily available on the study website or in databases or catalogues.
section.title: Discovery by potential users of the research/innovation data

fieldSet.title: Will you identify a data manager to manage your data, if not who will be responsible for the management of your data?
Identify the people or roles that will be responsible for the management of the project data

section.description: Identify who makes or will make the decision on whether to supply the data to a potential new user. Indicate whether the data will be deposited in and available from an identified community database, repository, archive or other infrastructure established to curate and share data.
section.title: Governance of access

fieldSet.title: What are the timescale/dependencies for when data will be accessible to others outside of your team?
fieldSet.extendedDescription: Summarize the principles of your current/intended policy.

section.description: UKRI’s requirement is for timely data sharing, with the understanding that a limited, defined period of exclusive use of data for primary research/innovation is reasonable according to the nature and value of the data, and that this restriction on sharing should be based on simple, clear principles.
section.title: The study team’s exclusive use of the data

fieldSet.title: Indicate whether external users are (will be) bound by data sharing agreements, setting out their main responsibilities.
Apart from the PI, who is responsible at your organisation/within your consortia for

page.title: Relevant institutional, departmental or study policies on data sharing and data security

fieldSet.title: Complete, policies which are relevant to your study, and are in the public domain, e.g. accessible through the internet. (Data Management Policy & Procedures, Data Security Policy, Data Sharing Policy, Institutional Information Policy)

section.title: Relevant institutional, departmental or study policies on data sharing and data security

page.title: Author of this Data Management Plan (Name) and, if different to that of the Principal Investigator, their telephone & email contact details

fieldSet.title: Is Author of this Data Management Plan different to that of the Principal Investigator?
Author of this Data Management Plan details

section.title: Author of this Data Management Plan (Name) and, if different to that of the Principal Investigator, their telephone & email contact details

field.textValue: The data is provided by central banks of countries and international organizations
The data is aimed to analyze the patterns that define the efficiency of monetary targeting in various countries/circumstances
the data sources are public and not subject to confidentiality
The data will be stored in a cloud-based repository

fieldSet.comment: Each time series will be marked as [country]-[indicator]
The metadata will provide the source and the type of gathered statistic

field.textValue: The data is monthly aggregated, provided as a non-balanced panel in CSV format
